body.title: Inference as comms channel: comms://model/* for LLM routing
body.result: InferenceTransport MVP at lab/utility_closet/transports/inference.py — inference/request → gateway call → inference/response with reply_to correlation via JSON envelope in content column (no schema migration yet). 6 tests pass. Follow-ups noted in commit message: schema migration for correlation columns, per-model hard routing, async response, CommsModule wiring. Commit fd69b3b1.
body.description:
Move the inference proxy into the utility closet as a comms transport. Every LLM call becomes a channel message.

## Design
- comms://model/claude-sonnet, comms://model/ollama-qwen, comms://model/openrouter-*
- Inference request = ChannelMessage with content_type=inference/request
- Inference response = ChannelMessage with content_type=inference/response, reply_to=request.id
- metadata carries: tokens_used, cost, model_id, latency_ms, finish_reason

## Benefits
- Every LLM call logged in same format as every other conversation
- Replay/debug inference by reading the channel log
- Budget tracking = read the channel, count messages, sum metadata.cost
- Model swapping = reroute channel, not code change
- Igor's tier system (local→OR→cloud) becomes routing rules on comms://model/* channels

## Transport: inference
- New transport type in lab/utility_closet/transports/inference.py
- Wraps existing inference_gateway.py routing logic
- Request/response via reply_to correlation ID

## Depends on
- T-uc-comms-module (comms infrastructure)
- T-uc-rack-architecture (module interface)
